name: MiMo-V2.5-Pro
archDisplay: Sparse MoE (384 routed experts, 8/token), 70 layers (1 dense + 69 MoE), hybrid Sliding-Window+Global attention 6:1 with learnable attention-sink bias, 3x MTP modules; text-only (no native vision/audio encoders)
note: Xiaomi's flagship coding/agentic model, 1.02T total / 42B active MoE, 1M context, fully open-sourced under MIT. Optimized for very long agentic trajectories (1,000+ tool calls); unlike sibling MiMo-V2.5, it is text-only (no native image/video/audio encoders).

results.notes: SWE-bench Verified; from the embedded fbCategories chart data on Xiaomi's official MiMo-V2.5-Pro product page. Independently corroborated (same 78.9 figure) via automatio.ai.
SWE-Bench Pro; from the embedded fbCategories/benchData chart arrays (consistent value in both blocks on the page).
Terminal-Bench 2.0; from the embedded benchmark chart data.
Humanity's Last Exam, with tool use (the page also reports 34.0 'w.o. tools'); from the embedded fbCategories chart data.

GDPVal-AA (labeled 'Elo' on the page, but the taxonomy's own unit for this slug is pts on a 0-2000 scale, which matches this value) from the 'General Agent' category of the embedded fbCategories chart data on Xiaomi's official MiMo-V2.5-Pro product page; MiMo-V2.5-Pro scores 1581 vs Claude Opus 4.6 1606, Gemini 3.1 Pro 1317, GPT-5.4 1674, Kimi K2.6 1480, GLM 5.1 1535, DeepSeek V4 Pro 1554, and the prior-gen MiMo-V2-Pro 1426. [score corrected during round-5 full-corpus accuracy re-verify: 1266 -> 1581]

GPQA Diamond, independently measured by Artificial Analysis. Not shown in AA's rendered (JS-hydrated) page text, but present as a raw field ('gpqa': 0.865656565656566) inside the model's embedded Next.js data payload fetched directly from the live page HTML. Corroborated verbatim by a third-party mirror, designforonline.com's MiMo-V2.5-Pro profile ('GPQA Diamond: 86.6%'). Not previously in the corpus.

IFBench (instruction-following), independently measured by Artificial Analysis (raw 'ifbench': 0.798639455782313 in the model's embedded JSON payload). Corroborated verbatim by designforonline.com's mirror ('IFBench: 79.9%'). New benchmark, not previously in the corpus for this model.

τ²-Bench Telecom, independently measured by Artificial Analysis (raw field 'tau2': 0.941520467836257 in the model's embedded JSON payload). AA's dedicated public leaderboard confirms this metric key specifically denotes '𝜏²-Bench Telecom' (https://artificialanalysis.ai/evaluations/tau2-bench), distinct from AA's newer 'τ³-Banking' evaluation which this same model reports separately (field 'tauBanking': 0.0866, no matching taxonomy slug, excluded). Corroborated by designforonline.com's identical mirror figure ('τ²-Bench: 94.2%'). Moderate-high confidence: the field is present and consistent across two sources, though AA's current UI surfaces τ³-Banking rather than τ²-Telecom for this model, suggesting the underlying eval may be a carried-over/legacy measurement rather than a freshly re-run one.

LiveCodeBench, independently measured by Vals AI. Verified via rendered screenshot ('81.35%'), since raw fetched HTML text shows a placeholder '0.0%' before the client-side animation completes; cross-checked against the CSS bar's 'target-width:81.354%' attribute in the raw HTML, which matches. New benchmark, not previously in the corpus for this model. Note: a separate, unrelated third-party aggregator (automatio.ai, echoed by SEO-content sites) claims a similar-looking 80.6% LiveCodeBench figure for this model, but the existing corpus file already flags that aggregator's whole benchmark table as likely-fabricated/unreliable -- this Vals AI figure is sourced independently from that flagged table and is not a reuse of it.

MMLU-Pro, independently measured by Vals AI. Verified via rendered screenshot ('84.59%'); raw HTML CSS bar 'target-width:84.595%' matches. New benchmark, not previously in the corpus for this model (the HF README's MMLU-Pro figure of 68.5 is explicitly for the pretrained Base checkpoint, not this released chat model, and was correctly excluded already).

SWE-bench Verified, independently re-measured by Vals AI using their own bash-tool-only mini-SWE-agent harness (Tier-2 independent evaluator per Vals AI's own methodology) -- distinct from Xiaomi's self-reported 78.9% already in this file. A harder/lower, less-favorable independent data point included deliberately for thoroughness. Verified via rendered screenshot ('74.00%'); raw HTML CSS bar 'target-width' matches.

Terminal-Bench 2.1, independently re-measured by Vals AI (own harness) -- distinct from Xiaomi's self-reported 68.4% already in this file. A notably harder/lower, less-favorable independent data point included deliberately for thoroughness rather than cherry-picking only favorable figures. Verified via rendered screenshot ('57.30%'); raw HTML CSS bar 'target-width:57.303%' matches.

Humanity's Last Exam, independently measured by Artificial Analysis without tool access (raw 'hle': 0.337812789620019 in the model's embedded JSON payload) -- distinct from the tool-augmented 48.0% already in this file's results. Closely matches Xiaomi's own self-reported 'w.o. tools' figure of 34.0% shown in the same chart data that produced the existing 48.0 entry, but that no-tools variant was never itself recorded as a separate result until now. A harder (no-tools) data point, included for thoroughness.

Official PinchBench (OpenClaw agent benchmark) leaderboard average score for MiMo-V2.5-Pro specifically (best score 89.5%), confirmed directly on PinchBench's own site via live fetch. Genuinely new: only the sibling MiMo-V2.5 (non-Pro) model currently has a pinchbench entry in this corpus (89.7); this is the first pinchbench entry for the Pro variant, and the two scores are close but distinct, consistent with them being separately-run evaluations of different models.

Text Arena (Overall) Elo on arena.ai, confirmed directly via live fetch of the 'mimo-v2.5-pro' row: rank 32, Elo 1466, +/-5 CI, 39,035 votes, MIT license, $0.43/$0.87 pricing, 1M context -- all matching this exact model. This figure was already referenced inside the sibling MiMo-V2.5 (non-Pro) file's own verificationNotes ('explicitly distinct on the same leaderboard from mimo-v2.5-pro at 1466') but was never itself recorded as a result row in the Pro model's own file until now -- genuinely missing entry, not a new discovery of the number itself.

verificationNotes: Params/architecture/context/license confirmed directly against the raw HuggingFace README (XiaomiMiMo/MiMo-V2.5-Pro). SWE-bench Verified score (78.9) independently cross-confirmed identical across three sources: Xiaomi's own product-page benchmark chart, a WebSearch snippet from automatio.ai, and a separate search-result summary — high confidence. IMPORTANT CAVEAT: the numeric benchmark table inside the HuggingFace README (GSM8K 99.6, GPQA-Diamond 66.7, MMLU 89.4, etc.) is explicitly labeled 'Base Model Evaluation' — i.e. it scores the pretrained MiMo-V2.5-Pro-Base checkpoint, not the released/post-trained chat model this entry represents — so those numbers were deliberately NOT used in 'results' below to avoid misrepresenting the shipped model. Instead, results are taken from the live JS-driven comparison charts embedded in Xiaomi's own MiMo-V2.5-Pro product page, fetched and parsed directly from raw HTML/JS (bypassing any summarization risk), which score the actual released model against Claude Opus 4.6, Gemini 3.1 Pro, GPT-5.4, Kimi K2.6, GLM 5.1, DeepSeek V4 Pro. A third-party aggregator (automatio.ai) also lists a much wider benchmark table (GPQA 54%, MMLU 86.7%, LiveCodeBench 80.6%, and even MMMU scores despite Pro being text-only) that conflicts substantially with both the vendor's own base-model table and the vendor's chat-model chart data, and separately implies multimodality Xiaomi itself denies for this model — treated as unreliable/likely-fabricated and excluded entirely.
